case_id: tooth #30 (lower right first molar implant site)

pi_items.source: Task description explicitly specifies output file path and format
Medical literature confirms 0.3 mm as measurement detection limit for clinical CT; PMC2868358, PLOS One article on cortical bone thickness measurement methods

Clinical studies show cortical bone thickness ranges from 0.3-4 mm typically; PMC2868358 explicitly states 0.3-4 mm range; ScienceDirect Cortical Thickness overview confirms 3-4 mm maximum for normal anatomy; 5.0 mm exceeds normal physiological range
Task description explicitly specifies three measurement depths: Crestal at 1mm, Mid-body at 5mm, Apical at 9mm below bone crest

privileged_info_summary: Task requires measuring buccal cortical bone thickness at tooth #30 (lower right first molar) in Blue Sky Plan CBCT imaging software at three standardized depths: crestal (1mm), mid-body (5mm), and apical (9mm) below the bone crest. Measurements should be saved to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\cortical_assessment.txt. Valid cortical bone thickness ranges from 0.3 mm (measurement detection limit) to approximately 4.0 mm based on clinical literature; the metadata claim of 5.0 mm maximum exceeds typical clinical measurements. Data source is real anonymized CBCT dental scans (DentalDICOM), not synthetic data.
